allusions.translator_note: Cicero's own coinage; not a quotation, but the lapidary cadence (six matched datives) makes it the most-quoted passage of the speech.

allusions.suspected_source: Polybius (oral tradition of Scipio Aemilianus's circle)
allusions.translator_note: Cicero invokes Scipio Aemilianus's well-attested literary patronage; the Polybian tradition is one likely source.
Multiple references to Ennius across the speech; section 22 cites the Annales by name.
Anecdote of Sulla rewarding a bad poet with a literary reward conditional on never writing again --- a piece of Sullan-period lore.

allusions.surface: Homerum Colophonii civem esse dicunt suum
allusions.translator_note: The 'seven cities of Homer' tradition; Cicero's catalogue (Smyrna, Chios, Colophon, Salamis, Rhodes, Argos, Athens) is the locus classicus.
